metadata.name: CSRS Retirement System Rules
metadata.description: Comprehensive rules for the Civil Service Retirement System (CSRS) including tiered annuity formula, eligibility, survivor benefits, CSRS Offset, voluntary contributions, sick leave credit, COLA rules, and special category provisions.
sources: 5 U.S.C. Chapter 83 — Civil Service Retirement System
Congressional Research Service: Federal Employees' Retirement System (RL30387)
notes: CSRS was closed to new entrants effective January 1, 1987 (replaced by FERS).
Approximately 100,000+ CSRS annuitants remain as of 2026.
CSRS employees generally are NOT covered by Social Security for their federal service.
CSRS provides a more generous annuity than FERS but has no TSP agency match and generally no Social Security.
CSRS Offset employees are the exception — they are covered by both CSRS and Social Security.

closed_note: Closed to new entrants. Employees hired on or after January 1, 1987 are covered by FERS.
social_security_note: CSRS employees do not pay Social Security (OASDI) tax on their federal earnings and generally do not earn SS credits for that service. WEP and GPO may apply if they have SS credits from non-federal employment.
employee_contribution_note: CSRS employees contribute 7.0% of basic pay toward their pension. This is higher than FERS (0.8%–4.4%) because CSRS provides a more generous benefit and no SS coverage.

annuity_formula.description: CSRS uses a tiered (weighted) formula that increases the accrual rate with years of service

tiers.formula: 0.02 × high-3 × remaining years and full months

annuity_formula.maximum_annuity_note: The basic annuity cannot exceed 80% of the high-3 average salary, except for amounts attributable to unused sick leave credit. This cap is typically reached at about 41 years and 11 months of service.

example.note: 56.25% of high-3 — compare to FERS at 30% (1% × 30) for the same service

high_3_average_pay.description: Highest average basic pay earned during any 3 consecutive years of service
includes: Annual premiums for standby duty or availability
high_3_average_pay.note: Usually the final 3 years before retirement, but can be an earlier period if pay was higher then

early_retirement.description: Under Voluntary Early Retirement Authority (VERA) or discontinued service
early_retirement.reduction: 2% per year under age 55 (1/6 of 1% per month)
early_retirement.reduction_note: This is a permanent reduction — it does not go away when the retiree turns 55

deferred_retirement.description: Separated before meeting immediate retirement eligibility
deferred_retirement.eligibility: At least 5 years of creditable civilian service
deferred_retirement.note: Must not have withdrawn retirement contributions. If contributions were refunded, rights are forfeited unless redeposit is made.

sick_leave_credit.description: Unused sick leave is added to creditable service for annuity computation purposes
sick_leave_credit.conversion: 2,087 hours = 1 year of service. Fractional months are dropped.

example.impact: At 2% tier, 6 months adds 1% of high-3 to the annuity

sick_leave_credit.note: Sick leave credit can push the annuity above the 80% cap. This is the only exception to the 80% maximum.

full_survivor.cost_note: Annuity is reduced by approximately 10% to provide full survivor benefit. Actual reduction is 2.5% of the first $3,600 plus 10% of the remainder.

partial_survivor.benefit_pct_note: Retiree can elect a survivor annuity based on any amount between $1 and the full annuity
partial_survivor.cost: Proportionally reduced from the full survivor cost

no_survivor.note: Requires spouse's notarized consent (SF 2801-2) to waive survivor annuity

survivor_annuity.former_spouse: Court orders can require survivor annuity for former spouse. This reduces the amount available for current spouse survivor election.

csrs_offset.description: CSRS Offset applies to employees who returned to federal service after a break and became covered by both CSRS and Social Security

contribution_rate_pct.note: CSRS Offset employees pay the full 6.2% Social Security tax plus a reduced CSRS contribution (0.2%) for a total of 6.4% — less than regular CSRS (7.0%)

csrs_offset.annuity_calculation: Calculated the same way as regular CSRS until age 62 (or retirement if later)

offset_at_62.description: At age 62, the CSRS annuity is reduced by the amount of Social Security benefit attributable to CSRS Offset service
offset_at_62.formula: Offset = lesser of: (a) SS benefit with federal earnings minus SS benefit without federal earnings, or (b) SS benefit × (Offset service years / 40)
offset_at_62.net_effect: Generally a wash — total income from OPM + SSA roughly equals the pre-offset CSRS annuity
offset_at_62.note: The offset occurs whether or not the retiree applies for Social Security. Therefore, retirees should apply for SS at 62.

voluntary_contributions.description: CSRS employees may make voluntary contributions to earn additional annuity
voluntary_contributions.eligibility: Active CSRS employees only (not available to FERS)
voluntary_contributions.minimum_per_pay_period: No minimum per pay period; total must be at least $25
voluntary_contributions.additional_annuity_rate: An additional annuity of $7 per year for every $100 in the voluntary contribution account (including interest)
voluntary_contributions.withdrawal: Can be withdrawn at any time as a lump sum (loses additional annuity)
voluntary_contributions.rollover: Can be rolled over to a Traditional IRA upon separation
voluntary_contributions.note: This is essentially a tax-deferred savings vehicle within CSRS. It is separate from TSP.

cola_rules.description: CSRS retirees receive full CPI-W COLA — the same as Social Security
cola_rules.comparison_to_fers: FERS COLA is capped (CPI-1% if CPI > 3%). CSRS gets the full COLA regardless of the CPI amount.
cola_rules.first_year_proration: The first COLA after retirement is prorated based on the number of months since retirement

special_category_employees.description: CSRS law enforcement officers and firefighters have enhanced provisions
special_category_employees.note: Special category includes law enforcement officers (LEO), firefighters (FF), and nuclear materials couriers. Air traffic controllers under CSRS have separate provisions.

deposit_service.description: Service for which no retirement contributions were withheld (pre-1989 temporary or certain intermittent service)
deposit_service.effect_if_unpaid: Service counts for eligibility but annuity is reduced for each unpaid deposit period
deposit_service.interest: Accrues after the deposit has been owed for more than 2 years

redeposit_service.description: Service for which retirement contributions were refunded upon separation
redeposit_service.effect_if_unpaid: Service counts for eligibility but cannot be used in annuity computation unless redeposit is made

tsp_access.description: CSRS employees may contribute to the TSP but receive NO agency automatic or matching contributions
tsp_access.contribution_limits: Same IRC § 402(g) limits as FERS employees
tsp_access.note: This is a significant disadvantage compared to FERS, where the government contributes up to 5% of pay via auto + match

no_srs.description: CSRS retirees do NOT receive the FERS Special Retirement Supplement
no_srs.reason: CSRS was designed as a standalone pension without Social Security integration. The SRS was created specifically for FERS to bridge the gap to SS at age 62.

wep_gpo_impact.description: CSRS retirees who also have Social Security credits from non-federal employment may be affected by WEP and GPO

wep.description: Windfall Elimination Provision reduces the Social Security benefit of workers who also receive a pension from non-covered employment
wep.applies_to: CSRS retirees who earned enough Social Security credits from private-sector or other covered employment

gpo.description: Government Pension Offset reduces Social Security spousal/survivor benefits by 2/3 of the government pension
gpo.applies_to: CSRS retirees who would otherwise be eligible for SS spousal or survivor benefits

wep_gpo_impact.repeal_status: As of March 2026, WEP and GPO have been repealed under the Social Security Fairness Act (P.L. 118-219), signed January 5, 2025. Repeal is retroactive to benefits payable after December 2023.
